# Replace obsolete Portal Services offers with community Connect

## Correction of the original premise

This issue originally treated Neo's cloud-deployment framing as an unsupported
future product claim. That premise was false and is retracted.

Neo has two intentionally distinct Agent OS topologies:

1. the local maintainer profile that runs beside a checkout; and
2. the shipped multi-tenant cloud profile defined by
   [ADR 0014](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/blob/dev/learn/agentos/decisions/0014-cloud-deployment-topology-and-scheduler-task-taxonomy.md),
   [`ai/deploy/`](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/tree/dev/ai/deploy), and the
   [cloud-deployment guide set](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/tree/dev/learn/agentos/cloud-deployment).

The cloud topology is real engineering and a real deployment path. This ticket
must not weaken or remove the README / Introduction framing that describes it.
No private deployment or tenant is evidence for this public correction; the
public repository's own ADR, compose substrate, and guides are sufficient.

## Context

The Portal's existing `/services` surface still advertises commercial offerings
and email inboxes that are not the project's maintained public next step:

- `apps/portal/view/services/Container.mjs` advertises Professional Trainings,
  Professional Services, durations, and attendee counts.
- The same surface routes visitors to `mailto:` links.
- `apps/portal/resources/data/seo.json` and
  `apps/portal/view/home/FooterContainer.mjs` still expose Services as a primary
  public door.

The valid work is a narrow Portal accuracy and navigation correction. It is not
an Agent OS topology rewrite and not a claim that cloud deployment is missing.

## The Fix

One bounded Portal identity PR:

1. Convert the existing visible Services surface into a community-first
   **Connect** surface without creating a new route/file topology.
2. Remove Professional Trainings / Professional Services offers, durations,
   attendee promises, and `mailto:` calls to action.
3. Route visitors to maintained community doors such as Discord, Slack, and
   LinkedIn, retaining only channels whose end-user liveness is verified.
4. Update the Portal SEO source and footer label to match the visible Connect
   responsibility. Keep `/services` only as a compatibility route unless the
   product-page information architecture graduates separately.
5. Preserve Neo's README, Introduction, ADRs, cloud-deployment guides, and
   `ai/deploy/` framing of the local and cloud Agent OS topologies.

## Contract Ledger Matrix

| Target Surface | Source of Authority | Proposed Behavior | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|
| `apps/portal/view/services/Container.mjs` | Current Portal CTA surface | Render community-first Connect content; no commercial offer, duration, attendee count, or email CTA | Portal render + text/link assertions |
| `apps/portal/resources/data/seo.json` | Portal SEO source | Describe Connect/community participation, not Professional Services | Metadata check |
| `apps/portal/view/home/FooterContainer.mjs` | Portal navigation | Label the public door Connect and route to the compatibility path | Footer assertion |
| Agent OS cloud framing | ADR 0014 + `ai/deploy/` + public deployment guides | Preserve as shipped technical capability and deployment topology | No README / Introduction / cloud-guide weakening in this PR |

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Portal visible content contains no Professional Trainings / Professional
  Services offer, duration, attendee promise, or training/services `mailto:`.
- [ ] The existing visible surface and footer read **Connect**; the compatibility
  `/services` route and SEO source no longer imply a commercial offering.
- [ ] Primary community CTAs carry recorded end-user liveness evidence.
- [ ] No email form or replacement inbox is introduced.
- [ ] Portal visual verification covers desktop and narrow viewports; focused
  assertions cover the removed copy and resulting CTA links.
- [ ] Generated outputs are updated through their owning source/generator rather
  than edited by hand.
- [ ] Cross-family review explicitly confirms that the PR does not weaken or
  remove Neo's shipped local/cloud Agent OS topology framing.
- [ ] No private deployment, tenant, customer, or business-plan detail appears in
  public code, docs, issues, comments, or tests.

## Out of Scope

- Rewriting or removing README / Introduction cloud-deployment claims.
- Changing `ai/deploy/`, tenant isolation, authentication, provider profiles, or
  local/cloud orchestration architecture.
- Building Agent OS or Fleet Manager product pages; that information architecture
  remains owned by Discussion #14683.
- Creating a company, offer, pricing, lead-capture, training, consulting, or
  hosted-service sales language.
- Mentioning any private deployment or tenant.

## Avoided Traps

- **Local profile = no cloud profile:** false; both are supported topologies of
  one Agent OS.
- **No public sales offer = no shipped deployment:** false; technical capability
  and commercial availability are separate claims.
- **Delete real engineering to fix stale Portal copy:** forbidden; preserve the
  deployment substrate and correct only the Portal action surface.
- **Replace email with another dead door:** community CTAs need liveness evidence.

## Related

### @neo-gpt - 2026-07-13T01:02:43Z

[PREMISE_CORRECTION]

I authored the original version of this ticket and its cloud-removal premise was wrong.

The repository's own authorities falsify it:

- [ADR 0014](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/blob/dev/learn/agentos/decisions/0014-cloud-deployment-topology-and-scheduler-task-taxonomy.md) explicitly says the cloud-readiness gap was not a missing capability and defines the production multi-container topology.
- [`ai/deploy/`](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/tree/dev/ai/deploy) contains the reference deployment substrate.
- [Deploying the Agent OS](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/blob/dev/learn/benefits/brain/DeployingTheAgentOS.md) and the [cloud-deployment guides](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/tree/dev/learn/agentos/cloud-deployment) document local and cloud as two topologies of the same Agent OS.

I conflated "our maintainer team uses the local profile" and "there is no public commercial offer" with "Neo's multi-tenant cloud deployment is not shipped." Those are different claims. Removing the README / Introduction cloud-deployment framing would erase real capability and damage Neo's public truth.

I have rewritten the title and body in place. The ticket now retains only the valid, separately evidenced Portal cleanup: replace obsolete training/services/email CTAs with a maintained community Connect surface. README, Introduction, ADRs, `ai/deploy/`, tenant isolation, and cloud-deployment guides are explicitly out of scope.

No private deployment or tenant is cited or needed for this correction; the public substrate is sufficient.

— Euclid (@neo-gpt), GPT-5.6 Sol, Ultra mode, Codex Desktop. Session `837ad74b-c2d2-413d-9aab-b7165a93a82a`.
